        o.customer_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS num_orders,
        SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_payment,
        MIN(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS first_purchase,
        MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_purchase
    FROM orders o
    INNER JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
    GROUP BY o.customer_id
),
customer_avg AS (

        customer_id,
        num_orders,
        total_payment / num_orders AS avg_payment_per_order,
        CASE

    FROM customer_metrics
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        num_orders,
        avg_payment_per_order,
        lifespan_weeks,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_payment_per_order DESC) AS rn
    FROM customer_avg
)

    num_orders AS number_of_orders,

WITH dates AS (
  SELECT player_id,
         CASE WHEN debut <= final_game THEN debut ELSE final_game END AS d1,
         CASE WHEN debut <= final_game THEN final_game ELSE debut END AS d2
  FROM player
  WHERE debut IS NOT NULL AND final_game IS NOT NULL
),
years_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2,
         CAST(strftime('%Y', d2) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', d1) AS INTEGER) -
         CASE WHEN strftime('%m%d', d2) < strftime('%m%d', d1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS yr
  FROM dates
),
anniversary AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr,
         date(d1, '+' || yr || ' years') AS anniv
  FROM years_calc
),
months_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr, anniv,
         (CAST(strftime('%m', d2) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%m', anniv) AS INTEGER) -
         CASE WHEN strftime('%d', d2) < strftime('%d', anniv) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + 12) % 12 AS mon
  FROM anniversary
),
month_start AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr, mon,
         date(anniv, '+' || mon || ' months') AS month_start
  FROM months_calc
),
days_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, yr, mon,
         CAST(julianday(d2) - julianday(month_start) AS INTEGER) AS dy
  FROM month_start
),
span AS (
  SELECT player_id,
         ROUND(CAST(yr AS REAL), 2) + ROUND(CAST(mon AS REAL)/12.0, 2) + ROUND(CAST(dy AS REAL)/365.0, 2) AS career_span
  FROM days_calc
)

FROM span

WITH RECURSIVE
dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('2017-01-01') AS dt
    UNION ALL
    SELECT DATE(dt, '+1 day')
    FROM dates
    WHERE dt < '2018-08-29'
),
daily_sales AS (
    SELECT d.dt AS sale_date,
           COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.product_category_name_english = 'toys' THEN oi.price ELSE 0 END), 0) AS total_sales
    FROM dates d
    LEFT JOIN orders o ON DATE(o.order_purchase_timestamp) = d.dt
    LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    LEFT JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    LEFT JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name
    GROUP BY d.dt
),
stats AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS n,
           SUM(day_num) AS sum_x,
           SUM(total_sales) AS sum_y,
           SUM(day_num * total_sales) AS sum_xy,
           SUM(day_num * day_num) AS sum_xx
    FROM (
        SELECT total_sales,
               julianday(sale_date) - julianday('2017-01-01') AS day_num
        FROM daily_sales
    )
),
coeff AS (
    SELECT (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) AS slope,
           (sum_y - ((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x)) * sum_x) / n AS intercept
    FROM stats
),
prediction_dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('2018-12-03') AS dt
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-04'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-05'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-06'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-07'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-08'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-09'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-10'
),
predicted_sales AS (
    SELECT dt,
           intercept + slope * (julianday(dt) - julianday('2017-01-01')) AS pred_sales
    FROM prediction_dates, coeff
),
moving_averages AS (
    SELECT p1.dt AS target_date,
           (SELECT AVG(p2.pred_sales)
            FROM predicted_sales p2
            WHERE julianday(p2.dt) BETWEEN julianday(p1.dt) - 2 AND julianday(p1.dt) + 2
           ) AS ma_5_symmetric
    FROM predicted_sales p1
    WHERE p1.dt IN ('2018-12-05', '2018-12-06', '2018-12-07', '2018-12-08')
)
SELECT SUM(ma_5_symmetric) AS sum_of_moving_averages
FROM moving_averages

-- Step 1: Define reference date for recency calculation (latest order purchase timestamp among delivered orders)
WITH reference_date AS (
    SELECT MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) AS max_date
    FROM orders
    WHERE order_status = 'delivered'
),
-- Step 2: Customer-level metrics (only delivered orders)
customer_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_unique_id,
        MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_purchase_date,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders,
        SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_spend
    FROM orders o
    JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
    JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id
),
-- Step 3: Add recency in days and average sales per order
customer_rfm_raw AS (
    SELECT
        cm.customer_unique_id,
        cm.total_orders,
        cm.total_spend,
        cm.total_spend / cm.total_orders AS avg_sales_per_order,
        (SELECT MAX(julianday(max_date)) - julianday(cm.last_purchase_date) FROM reference_date) AS recency_days
    FROM customer_metrics cm
),
-- Step 4: Assign RFM scores using quintiles (1 = best, 5 = worst)
rfm_scores AS (
    SELECT
        customer_unique_id,
        total_orders,
        total_spend,
        avg_sales_per_order,
        recency_days,
        -- R score: lower recency is better (most recent = 1)
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY recency_days ASC) AS r_score,
        -- F score: higher frequency is better
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY total_orders DESC) AS f_score,
        -- M score: higher monetary is better
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS m_score
    FROM customer_rfm_raw
),
-- Step 5: Create RFM segment label
rfm_segments AS (
    SELECT
        customer_unique_id,
        avg_sales_per_order,
        r_score || '-' || f_score || '-' || m_score AS rfm_segment
    FROM rfm_scores
)
-- Step 6: Report average sales per order per segment
SELECT
    rfm_segment,
    ROUND(AVG(avg_sales_per_order), 2) AS avg_sales_per_order_segment,
    COUNT(*) AS customer_count
FROM rfm_segments
GROUP BY rfm_segment
ORDER BY rfm_segment

WITH
-- Step 1: Get airport code for Abakan
abakan_code AS (
    SELECT a.airport_code
    FROM airports_data a
    WHERE json_extract(a.city, '$.en') = 'Abakan'
       OR json_extract(a.city, '$.ru') = 'Абакан'
),
-- Step 2: Get coordinates for all airports as numeric latitude/longitude
airport_coords AS (
    SELECT
        airport_code,
        CAST(
            SUBSTR(
                coordinates,
                INSTR(coordinates, ',') + 1,
                LENGTH(coordinates) - INSTR(coordinates, ',') - 1
            ) AS REAL
        ) AS latitude,
        CAST(
            SUBSTR(
                coordinates,
                2,
                INSTR(coordinates, ',') - 2
            ) AS REAL
        ) AS longitude
    FROM airports_data
),
-- Step 3: Join flights with departure and arrival airports
flights_with_coords AS (
    SELECT
        f.flight_id,
        f.departure_airport,
        f.arrival_airport,
        d.latitude AS dep_lat,
        d.longitude AS dep_lon,
        a.latitude AS arr_lat,
        a.longitude AS arr_lon
    FROM flights f
    JOIN airport_coords d ON f.departure_airport = d.airport_code
    JOIN airport_coords a ON f.arrival_airport = a.airport_code
    WHERE f.departure_airport IN (SELECT airport_code FROM abakan_code)
       OR f.arrival_airport IN (SELECT airport_code FROM abakan_code)
),
-- Step 4: Compute great-circle distance using Haversine formula (R = 6371 km)
distances AS (
    SELECT
        flight_id,
        6371 * 2 * ASIN(
            SQRT(
                POWER(SIN((RADIANS(arr_lat) - RADIANS(dep_lat)) / 2), 2)
                + COS(RADIANS(dep_lat))
                * COS(RADIANS(arr_lat))
                * POWER(SIN((RADIANS(arr_lon) - RADIANS(dep_lon)) / 2), 2)
            )
        ) AS distance_km
    FROM flights_with_coords
)
-- Step 5: Return the maximum distance
SELECT MAX(distance_km) AS distance_km
FROM distances

WITH
-- Step 1: Identify motorcycle collisions
mc_collisions AS (
    SELECT case_id
    FROM collisions
    WHERE motorcycle_collision = 1
),
-- Step 2: Get motorcyclist parties (drivers/passengers) with helmet status
motorcyclist_parties AS (
    SELECT 
        p.case_id,
        p.party_number,
        CASE 
            WHEN (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet used%' 
               OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet used%') THEN 1
            WHEN (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet not used%' 
               OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet not used%') THEN 0
            ELSE NULL
        END AS helmet_used
    FROM parties p
    INNER JOIN mc_collisions mc ON p.case_id = mc.case_id
    WHERE p.party_type IN ('driver', 'passenger')
      AND (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle%' 
        OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle%')
),
-- Step 3: Distinct collisions per helmet group
collisions_with_helmet AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT case_id
    FROM motorcyclist_parties
    WHERE helmet_used = 1
),
collisions_without_helmet AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT case_id
    FROM motorcyclist_parties
    WHERE helmet_used = 0
),
-- Step 4: Motorcyclist fatalities (killed victims matched to motorcyclist parties)
motorcyclist_fatalities AS (
    SELECT v.case_id
    FROM victims v
    INNER JOIN motorcyclist_parties mp 
        ON v.case_id = mp.case_id AND v.party_number = mp.party_number
    WHERE v.victim_degree_of_injury = 'killed'
),
-- Step 5: Aggregate statistics for each helmet group
stats_helmet AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) FROM collisions_with_helmet) AS total_collisions,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM motorcyclist_fatalities 
         WHERE case_id IN (SELECT case_id FROM collisions_with_helmet)) AS total_fatalities
),
stats_no_helmet AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) FROM collisions_without_helmet) AS total_collisions,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM motorcyclist_fatalities 
         WHERE case_id IN (SELECT case_id FROM collisions_without_helmet)) AS total_fatalities
)
-- Final output: two percentages rounded to two decimal places
SELECT
    ROUND(100.0 * sh.total_fatalities / NULLIF(sh.total_collisions, 0), 2) AS pct_fatalities_with_helmet,
    ROUND(100.0 * sn.total_fatalities / NULLIF(sn.total_collisions, 0), 2) AS pct_fatalities_without_helmet
FROM stats_helmet sh, stats_no_helmet sn

WITH airports AS (
  SELECT 
    airport_code,
    json_extract(city, '$.en') AS city_en,
    CAST(SUBSTR(coordinates, 2, INSTR(coordinates, ',')-2) AS REAL) AS lon,
    CAST(SUBSTR(coordinates, INSTR(coordinates, ',')+1, LENGTH(coordinates)-INSTR(coordinates, ',')-1) AS REAL) AS lat
  FROM airports_data
),
flight_cities AS (
  SELECT 
    f.flight_id,
    dp.city_en AS dep_city,
    dp.lon AS dep_lon,
    dp.lat AS dep_lat,
    ap.city_en AS arr_city,
    ap.lon AS arr_lon,
    ap.lat AS arr_lat
  FROM flights f
  JOIN airports dp ON f.departure_airport = dp.airport_code
  JOIN airports ap ON f.arrival_airport = ap.airport_code
  WHERE dp.city_en != ap.city_en
),
route_distances AS (
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN dep_city < arr_city THEN dep_city ELSE arr_city END AS city1,
    CASE WHEN dep_city < arr_city THEN arr_city ELSE dep_city END AS city2,
    2 * 6371 * ASIN(SQRT(
      POWER(SIN((arr_lat - dep_lat) * 3.141592653589793 / 360), 2) +
      COS(dep_lat * 3.141592653589793 / 180) * COS(arr_lat * 3.141592653589793 / 180) *
      POWER(SIN((arr_lon - dep_lon) * 3.141592653589793 / 360), 2)
    )) AS distance_km
  FROM flight_cities
),
city_pair_avg AS (
  SELECT 
    city1, 
    city2, 
    AVG(distance_km) AS avg_distance
  FROM route_distances
  GROUP BY city1, city2
),
ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN avg_distance < 1000 THEN 0
      WHEN avg_distance < 2000 THEN 1000
      WHEN avg_distance < 3000 THEN 2000
      WHEN avg_distance < 4000 THEN 3000
      WHEN avg_distance < 5000 THEN 4000
      WHEN avg_distance < 6000 THEN 5000
      ELSE 6000
    END AS distance_range,
    COUNT(*) AS pair_count
  FROM city_pair_avg
  GROUP BY distance_range
),
min_count AS (
  SELECT MIN(pair_count) AS min_pair_count
  FROM ranges
)
SELECT pair_count AS answer
FROM ranges, min_count
WHERE ranges.pair_count = min_count.min_pair_count

WITH bowler_balls AS (
    -- Count total deliveries bowled by each player
    SELECT bowler, COUNT(*) AS balls_bowled
    FROM ball_by_ball
    GROUP BY bowler
),
bowler_wickets AS (
    -- Count total wickets taken by each bowler
    SELECT b.bowler, COUNT(*) AS wickets
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN wicket_taken w 
      ON b.match_id = w.match_id 
     AND b.over_id = w.over_id 
     AND b.ball_id = w.ball_id 
     AND b.innings_no = w.innings_no
    GROUP BY b.bowler
),
bowler_strike_rate AS (
    -- Compute strike rate (balls per wicket); exclude bowlers with 0 wickets
    SELECT 
        bb.bowler,
        bb.balls_bowled,
        bw.wickets,
        CAST(bb.balls_bowled AS REAL) / bw.wickets AS strike_rate
    FROM bowler_balls bb
    JOIN bowler_wickets bw ON bb.bowler = bw.bowler
    WHERE bw.wickets > 0
)
-- Find the bowler with the lowest strike rate
SELECT p.player_name
FROM bowler_strike_rate bsr
JOIN player p ON bsr.bowler = p.player_id
ORDER BY bsr.strike_rate ASC
LIMIT 1

    SELECT match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id, SUM(extra_runs) AS extra_runs
    FROM extra_runs
    GROUP BY match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id
),
all_balls AS (
    -- All balls from ball_by_ball, left join bat and extra runs
    SELECT
        b.match_id,
        b.innings_no,
        b.over_id,
        b.ball_id,
        b.bowler,
        COALESCE(bs.runs_scored, 0) AS bat_runs,
        COALESCE(epb.extra_runs, 0) AS extra_runs
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored bs
        ON b.match_id = bs.match_id
        AND b.innings_no = bs.innings_no
        AND b.over_id = bs.over_id
        AND b.ball_id = bs.ball_id
    LEFT JOIN extra_per_ball epb
        ON b.match_id = epb.match_id
        AND b.innings_no = epb.innings_no
        AND b.over_id = epb.over_id
        AND b.ball_id = epb.ball_id
),
over_totals AS (
    -- Sum runs per over and get one bowler (all balls in an over share the same bowler)
    SELECT
        match_id,
        innings_no,
        over_id,
        MIN(bowler) AS bowler,  -- any ball's bowler works
        SUM(bat_runs + extra_runs) AS total_runs
    FROM all_balls
    GROUP BY match_id, innings_no, over_id
),
ranked_overs AS (
    -- Rank overs per match (across both innings) by total runs descending
    SELECT
        match_id,
        innings_no,
        over_id,
        bowler,
        total_runs,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY match_id ORDER BY total_runs DESC) AS rn
    FROM over_totals
),
highest_overs AS (
    -- Keep only the single highest over per match
    SELECT
        match_id,
        over_id,
        bowler,
        total_runs
    FROM ranked_overs
    WHERE rn = 1
)
-- Average of per-match highest over totals
SELECT AVG(total_runs) AS avg_highest_over_runs
FROM highest_overs

WITH
-- Step 1: Compute total runs conceded per over per match
per_over AS (
    SELECT
        bo.match_id,
        bo.over_id,
        bo.bowler,
        COALESCE(r.runs, 0) + COALESCE(e.extras, 0) AS total_runs
    FROM (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, MIN(bowler) AS bowler
        FROM ball_by_ball
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) bo
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, SUM(runs_scored) AS runs
        FROM batsman_scored
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) r ON bo.match_id = r.match_id AND bo.over_id = r.over_id
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, SUM(extra_runs) AS extras
        FROM extra_runs
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) e ON bo.match_id = e.match_id AND bo.over_id = e.over_id
),
-- Step 2: Find the maximum runs per match
match_max AS (
    SELECT match_id, MAX(total_runs) AS max_runs
    FROM per_over
    GROUP BY match_id
),
-- Step 3: Overs that achieved the maximum in their match
top_overs AS (
    SELECT po.*
    FROM per_over po
    JOIN match_max mm ON po.match_id = mm.match_id AND po.total_runs = mm.max_runs
),
-- Step 4: For each bowler, pick the over with the highest runs (if tie, smallest match_id)
bowler_best AS (
    SELECT
        bowler,
        match_id,
        total_runs,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY bowler ORDER BY total_runs DESC, match_id) AS rn
    FROM top_overs
)
-- Step 5: Get the top 3 bowlers by their best over's runs
SELECT
    bowler,
    match_id,
    total_runs AS runs_conceded
FROM bowler_best
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY total_runs DESC
LIMIT 3

WITH
-- Step 1: Monthly sales totals for 2019 and 2020, filtered by promo_total_id=1 and channel_total_id=1
monthly_sales AS (
    SELECT
        CAST(strftime('%Y', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS year,
        CAST(strftime('%m', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS month,
        SUM(s.amount_sold) AS total_sales
    FROM sales s
    JOIN promotions p ON s.promo_id = p.promo_id AND p.promo_total_id = 1
    JOIN channels c ON s.channel_id = c.channel_id AND c.channel_total_id = 1
    WHERE strftime('%Y', s.time_id) IN ('2019', '2020')
    GROUP BY year, month
),
-- Step 2: Separate 2019 and 2020 totals by month
sales_2019 AS (
    SELECT month, total_sales AS sales_2019
    FROM monthly_sales
    WHERE year = 2019
),
sales_2020 AS (
    SELECT month, total_sales AS sales_2020
    FROM monthly_sales
    WHERE year = 2020
),
-- Step 3: Compute growth rate (2020/2019) for each month where both exist and sales_2019 > 0
growth_rates AS (
    SELECT
        a.month,
        a.sales_2019,
        b.sales_2020,
        b.sales_2020 * 1.0 / a.sales_2019 AS growth_rate
    FROM sales_2019 a
    JOIN sales_2020 b ON a.month = b.month
    WHERE a.sales_2019 > 0
),
-- Step 4: Project 2021 monthly sales = 2020 sales * growth rate
projected_sales AS (
    SELECT
        g.month,
        g.sales_2020 * g.growth_rate AS projected_local
    FROM growth_rates g
),
-- Step 5: Convert projected sales to USD using France's exchange rate for 2021
converted_sales AS (
    SELECT
        p.month,
        p.projected_local * c.to_us AS projected_usd
    FROM projected_sales p
    JOIN currency c ON c.year = 2021 AND c.month = p.month AND c.country = 'France'
    WHERE c.to_us IS NOT NULL
),
-- Step 6: Compute median of the 12 monthly projected USD values
ordered_sales AS (
    SELECT
        projected_usd,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY projected_usd) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt
    FROM converted_sales
)
SELECT
    AVG(projected_usd) AS median_projected_sales_usd
FROM ordered_sales
WHERE rn IN ((cnt + 1) / 2, (cnt + 2) / 2)

WITH monthly_net AS (

        customer_id,
        strftime('%Y-%m', txn_date) AS month,
        SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'deposit' THEN txn_amount ELSE -txn_amount END) AS net_change
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id, month
),
customer_range AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(month) AS min_month,
        MAX(month) AS max_month
    FROM monthly_net
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
all_months AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        min_month AS month,
        max_month
    FROM customer_range
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        strftime('%Y-%m', date(substr(month,1,4) || '-' || substr(month,6,2) || '-01', '+1 month')),
        max_month
    FROM all_months
    WHERE month < max_month
),
filled_months AS (
    SELECT 
        a.customer_id,
        a.month,
        COALESCE(m.net_change, 0) AS net_change
    FROM all_months a

        ON a.customer_id = m.customer_id AND a.month = m.month
)

    SUM(net_change) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS closing_balance
FROM filled_months

WITH
-- Step 1: Find countries with exactly 9 distinct insertion days in January 2022
country_with_nine_days AS (
    SELECT country_code_2
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date LIKE '2022-01%'
    GROUP BY country_code_2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),

-- Step 2: Get distinct insertion dates for that country in January 2022
distinct_dates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT insert_date AS dt
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days LIMIT 1)
      AND insert_date LIKE '2022-01%'
),

-- Step 3: Assign a group number to each consecutive date sequence
-- Using the property: julianday(dt) - ROW_NUMBER() is constant for consecutive days
date_groups AS (
    SELECT dt,
           julianday(dt) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS grp
    FROM distinct_dates
),

-- Step 4: Compute each consecutive group's start, end, and length (days)
group_lengths AS (
    SELECT grp,
           MIN(dt) AS start_date,
           MAX(dt) AS end_date,
           COUNT(*) AS num_days
    FROM date_groups
    GROUP BY grp
),

-- Step 5: Select the longest consecutive period (break ties by earliest start)
longest_period AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM group_lengths
    ORDER BY num_days DESC, start_date ASC
    LIMIT 1
),

-- Step 6: All city entries for that country that fall within the longest consecutive period
entries_in_period AS (
    SELECT capital
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days LIMIT 1)
      AND insert_date >= (SELECT start_date FROM longest_period)
      AND insert_date <= (SELECT end_date FROM longest_period)
)

-- Step 7: Calculate proportion of capital entries (capital = 1)
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN capital = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS proportion_capital
FROM entries_in_period

WITH step1 AS (

    pco.rowid AS row_id,
    pco.order_id,
    pco.customer_id,
    pn.pizza_name,
    pco.exclusions,
    pco.extras,
    pr.toppings AS std_toppings,
    pco.order_time
  FROM pizza_customer_orders pco
  JOIN pizza_names pn ON pco.pizza_id = pn.pizza_id
  JOIN pizza_recipes pr ON pco.pizza_id = pr.pizza_id
),
std_toppings_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(std_toppings, ',', '","') || '"]')
  WHERE value != ''
),
exclusions_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(COALESCE(exclusions, ''), ',', '","') || '"]')
  WHERE value != ''
),
extras_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(COALESCE(extras, ''), ',', '","') || '"]')
  WHERE value != ''
),
final_toppings_count AS (
  SELECT row_id, topping_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM (
    SELECT st.row_id, st.topping_id
    FROM std_toppings_split st
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM exclusions_split es
      WHERE es.row_id = st.row_id AND es.topping_id = st.topping_id
    )
    UNION ALL
    SELECT ex.row_id, ex.topping_id
    FROM extras_split ex
  )
  GROUP BY row_id, topping_id
),
final_details AS (
  SELECT
    ft.row_id,
    ft.topping_id,
    ft.cnt,
    pt.topping_name
  FROM final_toppings_count ft
  JOIN pizza_toppings pt ON ft.topping_id = pt.topping_id
)
SELECT
  s.row_id,
  s.order_id,
  s.customer_id,
  s.pizza_name,
  s.pizza_name || ': ' ||
  COALESCE(
    (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(
      CASE WHEN fd.cnt > 1 THEN fd.cnt || 'x ' || fd.topping_name ELSE fd.topping_name END,
      ', '
      ORDER BY (CASE WHEN fd.cnt > 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END), fd.topping_name
    )
    FROM final_details fd
    WHERE fd.row_id = s.row_id
    GROUP BY fd.row_id),
    ''
  ) AS ingredients
FROM step1 s
GROUP BY s.row_id, s.order_id, s.pizza_name, s.order_time
ORDER BY s.row_id ASC

    im.index_value,
    im.composition / im.index_value AS ratio,

  WHERE (im._year = 2018 AND im._month >= 9) OR (im._year = 2019 AND im._month <= 8)
),
monthly_max AS (
  SELECT 
    _year,
    _month,
    month_year,
    interest_name,
    ratio AS max_index_composition,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY _year, _month ORDER BY ratio DESC) AS rn
  FROM ratio_data
),
top_monthly AS (

    _year,
    _month,
    month_year,
    interest_name,
    max_index_composition
  FROM monthly_max
  WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT 
  month_year,
  interest_name,
  max_index_composition,
  AVG(max_index_composition) OVER (ORDER BY _year, _month ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_avg,
  LAG(interest_name, 1) OVER (ORDER BY _year, _month) AS one_month_ago_interest,
  LAG(max_index_composition, 1) OVER (ORDER BY _year, _month) AS one_month_ago_value,
  LAG(interest_name, 2) OVER (ORDER BY _year, _month) AS two_months_ago_interest,

WITH
-- Step 1: Identify customers who placed orders in 1998
customers_1998 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT customerid
    FROM orders
    WHERE strftime('%Y', orderdate) = '1998'
),
-- Step 2: Compute total spending per customer (excluding discounts)
customer_totals AS (
    SELECT o.customerid, SUM(od.unitprice * od.quantity) AS total_spent
    FROM orders o
    INNER JOIN order_details od ON o.orderid = od.orderid
    WHERE strftime('%Y', o.orderdate) = '1998'
    GROUP BY o.customerid
),
-- Step 3: Count total customers in 1998 for percentage calculation
total_customers AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM customer_totals
),
-- Step 4: Assign each customer to a spending group using the thresholds
customer_groups AS (
    SELECT ct.customerid, ct.total_spent, cgt.groupname
    FROM customer_totals ct
    LEFT JOIN customergroupthreshold cgt
        ON ct.total_spent >= cgt.rangebottom AND ct.total_spent <= cgt.rangetop
)
-- Step 5: Aggregate by group and compute percentage
SELECT
    cg.groupname,
    COUNT(*) AS customer_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / tc.cnt, 2) AS percentage
FROM customer_groups cg
CROSS JOIN total_customers tc
GROUP BY cg.groupname
ORDER BY customer_count DESC

    WHERE sub.SubjectCode LIKE 'ENG%'

        WHEN cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows <= 0.6 THEN 'Third'

WITH transaction_spend AS (

  SELECT
    bt.member_id,
    bt.quantity,
    bp.price,
    bt.txn_date,
    CAST(SUBSTR(bt.txn_date, 7, 4) AS INTEGER) AS year,
    bt.quantity * bp.price AS spent
  FROM bitcoin_transactions bt
  JOIN bitcoin_prices bp
    ON bt.ticker = bp.ticker
    AND bt.txn_date = bp.market_date
  WHERE bt.ticker = 'BTC'
    AND bt.txn_type = 'BUY'
),
region_year_totals AS (
  -- Aggregate total spent and total quantity per region per year
  SELECT
    bm.region,
    ts.year,
    SUM(ts.spent) AS total_spent,
    SUM(ts.quantity) AS total_quantity
  FROM transaction_spend ts
  JOIN bitcoin_members bm ON ts.member_id = bm.member_id
  GROUP BY bm.region, ts.year
),
region_first_year AS (
  -- Identify the first purchase year for each region (to exclude later)
  SELECT
    region,
    MIN(year) AS first_year
  FROM region_year_totals
  GROUP BY region
),
filtered_yearly_avg AS (
  -- Exclude first year for each region and compute average purchase price
  SELECT
    ryt.region,
    ryt.year,
    ryt.total_spent / ryt.total_quantity AS avg_price
  FROM region_year_totals ryt
  JOIN region_first_year rfy
    ON ryt.region = rfy.region
    AND ryt.year > rfy.first_year
),
ranked AS (
  -- Rank regions by average price descending per year
  SELECT
    region,
    year,
    avg_price,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY avg_price DESC) AS rank
  FROM filtered_yearly_avg
),
with_pct_change AS (
  -- Calculate year-over-year percentage change for each region
  SELECT
    region,
    year,
    avg_price,
    rank,
    (avg_price - LAG(avg_price) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY year)) 
    / LAG(avg_price) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY year) * 100 AS pct_change
  FROM ranked
)
-- Final output ordered by year and region
SELECT
  year,
  region,
  ROUND(avg_price, 2) AS avg_purchase_price,
  rank,
  ROUND(pct_change, 2) AS annual_pct_change
FROM with_pct_change

WITH 
-- Step 1: Identify each legislator's first term start and the state from that term
first_term_rn AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id_bioguide ORDER BY term_start) AS rn
    FROM legislators_terms
),
first_terms AS (
    SELECT id_bioguide, term_start AS first_start, state
    FROM first_term_rn
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 2: Join with legislators to get gender and build the cohort of legislators with a first term
cohort AS (
    SELECT ft.id_bioguide,
           ft.first_start,
           ft.state,
           lg.gender
    FROM first_terms ft
    JOIN legislators lg ON ft.id_bioguide = lg.id_bioguide
),
-- Step 3: Keep only states that have at least one male AND one female legislator
state_gender_counts AS (
    SELECT state, gender, COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM cohort
    GROUP BY state, gender
),
eligible_states AS (
    SELECT state
    FROM state_gender_counts
    GROUP BY state
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT gender) = 2 AND MIN(cnt) > 0
),
cohort_eligible AS (
    SELECT c.*
    FROM cohort c
    WHERE c.state IN (SELECT state FROM eligible_states)
),
-- Step 4: Define the six time offsets
offsets(off) AS (
    VALUES (0), (2), (4), (6), (8), (10)
),
-- Step 5: Build all (legislator, offset) pairs and compute the check date (Dec 31 of the year)
check_dates AS (
    SELECT ce.id_bioguide,
           ce.state,
           ce.gender,
           o.off,
           printf('%d-12-31',
                  CAST(strftime('%Y', ce.first_start) AS INTEGER) + o.off
           ) AS check_date
    FROM cohort_eligible ce
    CROSS JOIN offsets o
),
-- Step 6: For each (state, gender, offset) count how many distinct legislators have a term covering that check date
retention_raw AS (
    SELECT cd.state,
           cd.gender,
           cd.off,
           COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN lt.id_bioguide IS NOT NULL THEN cd.id_bioguide END) AS retained_count
    FROM check_dates cd
    LEFT JOIN legislators_terms lt
        ON cd.id_bioguide = lt.id_bioguide
        AND lt.term_start <= cd.check_date
        AND lt.term_end >= cd.check_date
    GROUP BY cd.state, cd.gender, cd.off
),
-- Step 7: For each state and gender, count number of offsets where retention > 0
state_gender_ok AS (
    SELECT state, gender,
           SUM(CASE WHEN retained_count > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS offsets_ok
    FROM retention_raw
    GROUP BY state, gender
)
-- Step 8: Keep states where both genders have all 6 offsets with retention > 0
SELECT state
FROM state_gender_ok
GROUP BY state
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT gender) = 2 AND MIN(offsets_ok) = 6
ORDER BY state

WITH

rated_above_8 AS (
    SELECT movie_id
    FROM ratings
    WHERE avg_rating > 8
),

-- Step 2: Top 3 genres with the most movies rated above 8
genre_counts AS (
    SELECT g.genre, COUNT(DISTINCT g.movie_id) AS movie_count
    FROM genre g
    INNER JOIN rated_above_8 r ON g.movie_id = r.movie_id
    GROUP BY g.genre
    ORDER BY movie_count DESC
    LIMIT 3
),

-- Step 3: Movies that belong to any of the top genres and are rated above 8
top_genre_movies AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT g.movie_id
    FROM genre g
    INNER JOIN rated_above_8 r ON g.movie_id = r.movie_id
    INNER JOIN genre_counts gc ON g.genre = gc.genre
),

-- Step 4: Count movies directed per director for those movies, get top 4
director_counts AS (
    SELECT dm.name_id, COUNT(DISTINCT dm.movie_id) AS movie_count
    FROM director_mapping dm
    INNER JOIN top_genre_movies t ON dm.movie_id = t.movie_id
    GROUP BY dm.name_id
    ORDER BY movie_count DESC, dm.name_id
    LIMIT 4
)

-- Step 5: Retrieve director names
SELECT n.name AS director_name, dc.movie_count
FROM director_counts dc
INNER JOIN names n ON dc.name_id = n.id

  ),

        MAX(CASE WHEN ms.model != 'Stack' THEN ms.test_score END) AS max_non_stack
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT name, version, step FROM model_score) AS s
    LEFT JOIN model_score ms ON ms.name = s.name AND ms.version = s.version AND ms.step = s.step

    SELECT id
    FROM packaging
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT contains_id FROM packaging_relations)

-- Leaf-level packaging items (those that do not contain any other packaging)
leaves AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM packaging
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT packaging_id FROM packaging_relations)
),
-- Recursive expansion of all root-to-leaf paths
paths AS (
    SELECT r.id AS root_id, r.id AS current_id, 1 AS qty_product
    FROM roots r

    SELECT p.root_id, pr.contains_id, p.qty_product * pr.qty
    FROM paths p
    JOIN packaging_relations pr ON p.current_id = pr.packaging_id
),
-- Total leaf quantity per root combination
leaf_totals AS (
    SELECT p.root_id, SUM(p.qty_product) AS total_leaf_qty
    FROM paths p
    WHERE p.current_id IN (SELECT id FROM leaves)
    GROUP BY p.root_id
)

FROM leaf_totals

-- This query computes the picking instructions for order 423.
-- It uses a range-based allocation method to assign inventory to order lines,
-- respecting the sequence of order lines (by rowid) and FIFO + smallest-quantity-first prioritization.
WITH
-- Step 1: Extract order lines for order 423, assign a sequence using rowid
order_lines AS (
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        ol.qty,
        ol.rowid AS seq
    FROM orderlines ol
    WHERE ol.order_id = 423
),

-- Step 2: For each product, compute cumulative required quantities over order lines
ordered_lines AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        qty,
        seq,
        COALESCE(
            SUM(qty) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY seq ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING),
            0
        ) AS cum_req_start,
        SUM(qty) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY seq) AS cum_req_end
    FROM order_lines
),

-- Step 3: Get inventory for the products in order 423, located in warehouse 1
-- Join with purchases for date, and locations for aisle/position.
-- Compute cumulative inventory amounts, ordering by purchased (ASC) then quantity (ASC) then inventory.id (stable tiebreak).
inventory_data AS (
    SELECT
        i.product_id,
        i.qty,
        i.id AS inv_id,
        l.aisle,
        l.position,
        p.purchased,
        COALESCE(
            SUM(i.qty) OVER (PARTITION BY i.product_id ORDER BY p.purchased, i.qty, i.id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING),
            0
        ) AS cum_inv_start,
        SUM(i.qty) OVER (PARTITION BY i.product_id ORDER BY p.purchased, i.qty, i.id) AS cum_inv_end
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN purchases p ON i.purchase_id = p.id
    JOIN locations l ON i.location_id = l.id
    WHERE l.warehouse = 1
      AND i.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM order_lines)
),

-- Step 4: Identify overlapping intervals between order lines and inventory
-- Compute the quantity to pick from each inventory record for each order line.
allocations AS (
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        ol.cum_req_start,
        ol.cum_req_end,
        inv.inv_id,
        inv.aisle,
        inv.position,
        inv.cum_inv_start,
        inv.cum_inv_end,
        -- Overlap length
        CASE
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end < inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start > inv.cum_inv_start THEN ol.cum_req_end - ol.cum_req_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end >= inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start <= inv.cum_inv_start THEN inv.cum_inv_end - inv.cum_inv_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end < inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start <= inv.cum_inv_start THEN ol.cum_req_end - inv.cum_inv_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end >= inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start > inv.cum_inv_start THEN inv.cum_inv_end - ol.cum_req_start
            ELSE 0
        END AS pick_qty
    FROM ordered_lines ol
    JOIN inventory_data inv ON ol.product_id = inv.product_id
    WHERE ol.cum_req_start < inv.cum_inv_end
      AND ol.cum_req_end > inv.cum_inv_start
),

-- Step 5: Aggregate picks per product and per location (sum over order lines)
picking_instructions AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        aisle,
        position,
        SUM(pick_qty) AS total_pick_qty
    FROM allocations
    WHERE pick_qty > 0
    GROUP BY product_id, aisle, position
)

-- Final output: product IDs, aisles, and positions (and optionally the quantity)
-- According to the question, only the three columns are requested.
-- Uncomment the total_pick_qty if quantities are also desired.
SELECT
    product_id,
    aisle,
    position
    -- , total_pick_qty   -- uncomment to include the quantity to pick from each location
FROM picking_instructions
ORDER BY product_id, aisle, position

WITH
-- Combine inventory and order events into a single timeline per product
events AS (
    SELECT
        i.product_id,
        p.purchased AS event_date,
        -- Sorting: 0 for inventory (so it comes before orders on the same date)
        0 AS event_type_sort,
        i.qty AS qty,
        i.id AS source_id,
        1 AS is_inventory,
        i.qty AS inv_qty,
        NULL AS order_qty
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN purchases p ON i.purchase_id = p.id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        o.ordered AS event_date,
        -- Sorting: 1 for orders (after inventory on same date)
        1 AS event_type_sort,
        ol.qty AS qty,
        o.id AS source_id,
        0 AS is_inventory,
        NULL AS inv_qty,
        ol.qty AS order_qty
    FROM orderlines ol
    JOIN orders o ON ol.order_id = o.id
),
-- Add a global event order per product respecting FIFO rules
events_with_order AS (
    SELECT *,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_date,
                     event_type_sort,
                     CASE WHEN is_inventory = 1 THEN qty ELSE NULL END,  -- smallest qty first
                     source_id
        ) AS event_rank
    FROM events
),
-- Compute cumulative inventory and cumulative demand before each event
cumulative AS (
    SELECT *,
        COALESCE(SUM(inv_qty) OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_rank
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ), 0) AS cum_inv_before,
        COALESCE(SUM(order_qty) OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_rank
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ), 0) AS cum_demand_before
    FROM events_with_order
),
-- For each order event, compute the picked quantity using FIFO
order_alloc AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        source_id AS order_id,
        order_qty,
        event_date,
        cum_inv_before,
        cum_demand_before,
        CASE
            WHEN cum_inv_before - cum_demand_before <= 0 THEN 0
            ELSE MIN(order_qty, cum_inv_before - cum_demand_before)
        END AS picked_qty
    FROM cumulative
    WHERE is_inventory = 0  -- only order rows
),
-- Compute pick percentage per order line
order_pct AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        order_id,
        1.0 * picked_qty / order_qty AS pick_pct
    FROM order_alloc
    WHERE order_qty > 0
)
-- Final aggregation by product name
SELECT
    pg.name AS product_name,
    AVG(op.pick_pct) AS avg_pick_percentage
FROM order_pct op
JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.id
JOIN product_groups pg ON p.group_id = pg.id
GROUP BY pg.name
ORDER BY pg.name

WITH RECURSIVE months(mth) AS (

    FROM months 
    WHERE mth < '2019-01-01'
),

    SELECT 4160 AS product_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 7790
),
base AS (
    SELECT 
        p.product_id,
        m.mth,
        COALESCE(s.qty, 0.0) AS qty
    FROM months m
    CROSS JOIN target_products p
    LEFT JOIN monthly_sales s 
        ON s.product_id = p.product_id 
        AND s.mth = m.mth
),
with_time AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        mth,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY mth) AS time_step
    FROM base
),
seasonal_data AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        CAST(strftime('%m', mth) AS INTEGER) AS month_num,
        AVG(qty) AS avg_month,
        (SELECT AVG(qty) FROM with_time WHERE product_id = t.product_id AND time_step BETWEEN 7 AND 30) AS grand_avg
    FROM with_time t
    WHERE time_step BETWEEN 7 AND 30
    GROUP BY product_id, month_num
),
seasonal_factors AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        month_num,
        CASE WHEN grand_avg = 0 THEN 1 ELSE avg_month / grand_avg END AS season_factor
    FROM seasonal_data
),
deseq AS (
    SELECT 
        t.product_id,
        t.time_step,
        t.qty,
        COALESCE(sf.season_factor, 1) AS season_factor,
        CASE

            THEN t.qty / COALESCE(sf.season_factor, 1) 
            ELSE t.qty 
        END AS deseasonalized
    FROM with_time t
    JOIN seasonal_factors sf 
        ON sf.product_id = t.product_id 
        AND sf.month_num = CAST(strftime('%m', t.mth) AS INTEGER)
),
regression AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        SUM(time_step) AS sum_w,
        SUM(time_step * time_step) AS sum_wx,
        SUM(time_step * deseasonalized) AS sum_wy,
        SUM(time_step * time_step * time_step) AS sum_wx2,
        SUM(time_step * time_step * deseasonalized) AS sum_wxy
    FROM deseq
    GROUP BY product_id
),
coeffs AS (

        (sum_wy - ((sum_w * sum_wxy - sum_wx * sum_wy) * 1.0 / 
            NULLIF((sum_w * sum_wx2 - sum_wx * sum_wx), 0)) * sum_wx) / 
            NULLIF(sum_w, 0) AS intercept
    FROM regression

    SELECT product_id, time_step

WITH

    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        txn_date,
        SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'deposit' THEN txn_amount ELSE -txn_amount END) AS net
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id, txn_date
),
-- Step 2: Earliest and latest transaction dates per customer
customer_date_range AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(txn_date) AS min_date,
        MAX(txn_date) AS max_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
-- Step 3: Generate every date between min and max for each customer using recursive CTE
date_series AS (
    SELECT customer_id, min_date AS date, max_date
    FROM customer_date_range
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        d.customer_id, 
        DATE(d.date, '+1 day') AS date,
        d.max_date
    FROM date_series d
    WHERE DATE(d.date, '+1 day') <= d.max_date
),
-- Step 4: Attach daily net (0 if no transaction) to the date series
daily_with_net AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.customer_id, 
        ds.date, 
        COALESCE(dn.net, 0) AS net
    FROM date_series ds
    LEFT JOIN daily_net dn 
        ON ds.customer_id = dn.customer_id AND ds.date = dn.txn_date
),
-- Step 5: Compute daily balance recursively, applying floor at zero
balance_cte AS (
    -- Anchor: first day for each customer (previous balance = 0)
    SELECT 
        customer_id, 
        date, 
        net, 
        CASE WHEN net < 0 THEN 0 ELSE net END AS balance
    FROM daily_with_net
    WHERE date = (SELECT min_date FROM customer_date_range cd WHERE cd.customer_id = daily_with_net.customer_id)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive step: balance = max(0, previous_balance + current net)
    SELECT 
        dw.customer_id, 
        dw.date, 
        dw.net,
        CASE WHEN bc.balance + dw.net < 0 THEN 0 ELSE bc.balance + dw.net END AS balance
    FROM balance_cte bc
    JOIN daily_with_net dw 
        ON bc.customer_id = dw.customer_id 
        AND DATE(dw.date, '-1 day') = bc.date
)

    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        SUBSTR(date, 1, 7) AS month,
        MAX(balance) AS max_balance
    FROM balance_cte
    GROUP BY customer_id, month
)

SELECT 
    month,
    SUM(max_balance) AS total_max_balance
FROM monthly_max
GROUP BY month

        ) AS net_amount
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id, txn_date

customer_dates AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        MIN(txn_date) AS min_date,
        MAX(txn_date) AS max_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
),

    SELECT customer_id, min_date AS date
    FROM customer_dates
    UNION ALL
    SELECT ds.customer_id, DATE(ds.date, '+1 day')
    FROM date_series ds
    JOIN customer_dates cd ON ds.customer_id = cd.customer_id
    WHERE ds.date < cd.max_date
),

daily_balance AS (
    SELECT
        ds.customer_id,
        ds.date,
        COALESCE(dn.net_amount, 0) AS net_today,
        SUM(COALESCE(dn.net_amount, 0)) OVER (
            PARTITION BY ds.customer_id
            ORDER BY ds.date
            ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING

    LEFT JOIN daily_net dn ON ds.customer_id = dn.customer_id AND ds.date = dn.txn_date

  FROM years

-- Classify each week as "before" (4 weeks prior) or "after" (4 weeks after)
period_sales AS (
  SELECT 
    md.y,
    CASE 
      WHEN cws.week_date >= date(md.monday, '-28 days') 
       AND cws.week_date < md.monday THEN 'before'
      WHEN cws.week_date > md.monday 
       AND cws.week_date <= date(md.monday, '+28 days') THEN 'after'
    END AS period,
    cws.sales
  FROM cleaned_weekly_sales cws
  JOIN monday_dates md ON cws.calendar_year = md.y
  WHERE (cws.week_date >= date(md.monday, '-28 days') AND cws.week_date < md.monday)
     OR (cws.week_date > md.monday AND cws.week_date <= date(md.monday, '+28 days'))

  FROM period_sales

WITH

    SELECT session, path AS landing_page
    FROM (

        FROM activity_log
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 2: For each session, identify the last page (exit) based on latest stamp
last_page AS (
    SELECT session, path AS exit_page
    FROM (
        SELECT session, path, stamp,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp DESC) AS rn
        FROM activity_log
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 3: Combine unique (session, page) pairs where the page is either landing or exit
combined AS (
    SELECT session, landing_page AS page FROM first_page
    UNION
    SELECT session, exit_page AS page FROM last_page
)
-- Step 4: Count distinct sessions per web page

FROM combined
GROUP BY page
ORDER BY page

WITH pos AS (

),
pos_with_prev AS (

  FROM pos

gains AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap_num, position, prev_pos,

retirements_per_lap AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, CAST(lap AS INT) AS lap_num

retirement_prev_pos AS (
  SELECT r.race_id, r.driver_id AS retired_driver, r.lap_num,

),
retirement_caused AS (

),
pit_stop_caused AS (
  SELECT g.race_id, g.driver_id, g.lap_num, g.total_gain, g.prev_pos,
         g.retirement_count,

  FROM retirement_caused g
  LEFT JOIN pit_stop_prev_pos p
         ON g.race_id = p.race_id AND g.lap_num = p.lap_num

FROM categorized

FROM categorized

WITH
    -- Step 1: Select races that have pit stop data available
    races_with_pit AS (
        SELECT race_id
        FROM races_ext
        WHERE is_pit_data_available = '1'
    ),

    -- Step 2: Enrich lap positions with driver details, pit-stop flags and retirement flags
    lap_enriched AS (
        SELECT
            l.race_id,
            l.driver_id,
            d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS driver_name,
            l.lap,
            l.position,
            l.lap_type,
            CASE WHEN p.driver_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_pit_stop,
            CASE WHEN r.driver_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_retirement
        FROM lap_positions l
        JOIN drivers d ON l.driver_id = d.driver_id
        JOIN races_with_pit rp ON l.race_id = rp.race_id
        LEFT JOIN pit_stops p
            ON p.race_id = l.race_id AND p.driver_id = l.driver_id AND p.lap = l.lap
        LEFT JOIN retirements r
            ON r.race_id = l.race_id AND r.driver_id = l.driver_id AND CAST(r.lap AS INTEGER) = l.lap
    ),

    -- Step 3: For each driver and race, bring the previous lap's position and flags
    laps_with_prev AS (
        SELECT
            race_id,
            driver_id,
            driver_name,
            lap,
            position,
            lap_type,
            has_pit_stop,
            has_retirement,
            LAG(lap) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_lap,
            LAG(position) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_position,
            LAG(has_pit_stop) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_has_pit_stop,
            LAG(has_retirement) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_has_retirement
        FROM lap_enriched
    ),

    -- Step 4: Keep only consecutive laps (previous lap exists and exactly one less)
    consecutive_laps AS (
        SELECT
            race_id,
            driver_id,
            driver_name,
            lap AS curr_lap,
            position AS curr_pos,
            prev_position AS prev_pos,
            has_pit_stop AS curr_pit,
            has_retirement AS curr_ret,
            prev_has_pit_stop AS prev_pit,
            prev_has_retirement AS prev_ret,
            lap_type
        FROM laps_with_prev
        WHERE prev_lap IS NOT NULL AND prev_lap = lap - 1
    ),

    -- Step 5: Detect overtakes between pairs of drivers on the same lap
    overtake_events AS (
        SELECT
            a.race_id,
            a.curr_lap,
            a.driver_name AS driver_a,
            b.driver_name AS driver_b,
            a.curr_pos AS pos_a_curr,
            b.curr_pos AS pos_b_curr,
            a.prev_pos AS pos_a_prev,
            b.prev_pos AS pos_b_prev,
            a.curr_pit AS a_pit,
            b.curr_pit AS b_pit,
            a.curr_ret AS a_ret,
            b.curr_ret AS b_ret,
            a.prev_pit AS a_prev_pit,
            b.prev_pit AS b_prev_pit,
            a.prev_ret AS a_prev_ret,
            b.prev_ret AS b_prev_ret,
            a.lap_type
        FROM consecutive_laps a
        JOIN consecutive_laps b
            ON a.race_id = b.race_id
            AND a.curr_lap = b.curr_lap
            AND a.driver_id < b.driver_id
        WHERE 
            -- driver a was ahead on previous lap and behind on current lap => overtaken by b
            (a.prev_pos < b.prev_pos AND a.curr_pos > b.curr_pos)
            OR
            -- driver b was ahead on previous lap and behind on current lap => overtaken by a
            (b.prev_pos < a.prev_pos AND b.curr_pos > a.curr_pos)
    ),

    -- Step 6: Classify each overtake and count
    classified AS (
        SELECT
            CASE
                WHEN curr_lap = 1 THEN 'Start Overtake'
                WHEN a_pit = 1 OR b_pit = 1 OR a_prev_pit = 1 OR b_prev_pit = 1 THEN 'Pit Stop Overtake'
                WHEN a_ret = 1 OR b_ret = 1 OR a_prev_ret = 1 OR b_prev_ret = 1 THEN 'Retirement Overtake'
                ELSE 'On-Track Overtake'
            END AS overtake_type,
            1 AS cnt
        FROM overtake_events
        -- Ensure no retirement or pit stop on the current lap for the overtaking driver? Already filtered by CASE.
    )

-- Final result
SELECT
    overtake_type,
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_overtakes
FROM classified
GROUP BY overtake_type
ORDER BY overtake_type

WITH driver_years AS (
    -- Drivers who raced in the 1950s and drove for exactly one constructor in a year (from team_driver_ranks)
    SELECT driver_id, year
    FROM team_driver_ranks
    WHERE year BETWEEN 1950 AND 1959
    GROUP BY driver_id, year
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT constructor_id) = 1
),
race_participation AS (
    -- All races in the 1950s with driver participation
    SELECT res.driver_id, ra.year, ra.race_id, ra.round
    FROM results res
    JOIN races ra ON res.race_id = ra.race_id
    WHERE ra.year BETWEEN 1950 AND 1959
),
season_races AS (
    -- Drivers who participated in at least 2 distinct rounds in a year
    SELECT driver_id, year, COUNT(DISTINCT round) AS num_races
    FROM race_participation
    GROUP BY driver_id, year
    HAVING num_races >= 2
)
SELECT DISTINCT d.forename, d.surname
FROM driver_years dy
JOIN season_races sr ON dy.driver_id = sr.driver_id AND dy.year = sr.year
JOIN drivers d ON dy.driver_id = d.driver_id
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename

WITH active_drivers AS (

    FROM results
    JOIN races r ON results.race_id = r.race_id

all_driver_year_races AS (
    SELECT ad.driver_id, ad.year, r.round, r.race_id,
           res.constructor_id,
           CASE WHEN res.race_id IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_missing
    FROM active_drivers ad
    JOIN races r ON ad.year = r.year
    LEFT JOIN results res ON res.race_id = r.race_id AND res.driver_id = ad.driver_id
),
missed_totals AS (
    SELECT *,
           SUM(is_missing) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year) AS total_missed_in_year
    FROM all_driver_year_races
),
lag_lead AS (
    SELECT *,
           LAG(constructor_id) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS prev_constructor,
           LEAD(constructor_id) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS next_constructor
    FROM missed_totals
    WHERE total_missed_in_year < 3
),
missing_with_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           CASE WHEN is_missing = 1 AND 
                     (LAG(is_missing,1,0) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) = 0)
                THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_start
    FROM lag_lead
),
missing_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           SUM(is_start) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS grp
    FROM missing_with_groups
    WHERE is_missing = 1
),
numbered_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year, grp ORDER BY round) AS rn_in_group,
           COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year, grp) AS group_size
    FROM missing_groups
),
gap_details AS (
    SELECT driver_id, year, grp,
           MIN(round) AS first_round,
           MAX(round) AS last_round,
           MAX(CASE WHEN rn_in_group = 1 THEN prev_constructor END) AS constructor_before,
           MAX(CASE WHEN rn_in_group = group_size THEN next_constructor END) AS constructor_after
    FROM numbered_groups
    GROUP BY driver_id, year, grp
)
SELECT 
    AVG(first_round) AS avg_first_round_of_missed,
    AVG(last_round) AS avg_last_round_of_missed
FROM gap_details

WITH race_laps AS (

    FROM pit_stops

changes AS (

           r1.lap AS from_lap,
           r1.position AS old_pos,
           r2.position AS new_pos
    FROM race_laps r1

        ON r1.race_id = r2.race_id
        AND r1.driver_id = r2.driver_id
        AND r1.lap = r2.lap - 1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM pit_laps p
        WHERE p.race_id = r1.race_id

    )

FROM (
    SELECT driver_id,
           SUM(CASE WHEN new_pos > old_pos THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overtaken,
           SUM(CASE WHEN new_pos < old_pos THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overtakes

WITH first_event AS (

        path AS first_path,

    FROM (
        SELECT 
            session,

        FROM activity_log
        WHERE path IN ('/detail', '/complete')
    ) t

),
pre_events_count AS (

        fe.session,
        fe.first_path,
        fe.first_search_type,
        COUNT(al.stamp) AS cnt

        AND al.search_type IS NOT NULL 
        AND al.search_type != ''
        AND al.stamp < fe.first_stamp
    GROUP BY fe.session, fe.first_path, fe.first_search_type
),

    -- Find the minimum count among all sessions

    pec.first_path AS path,
    pec.first_search_type AS search_type
FROM pre_events_count pec
